RXT.headline: Why Rackspace Rallied Today
RXT.description: Rackspace stock surged up to 21% after announcing a partnership expansion with AMD to deploy 30 MW of compute capacity for enterprise customers in regulated industries through 2028. The company also announced a 15% workforce reduction to save $75-85 million annually and refocus on AI opportunities. Despite the rally, Rackspace remains well below its $21 IPO price from 2020.

RXT.headline: Why Is Rackspace Stock Surging Today?
RXT.description: Rackspace Technology (RXT) shares surged 11.41% after announcing a definitive agreement with AMD for phased deployment of 30 megawatts of AMD-based AI compute infrastructure. The partnership formalizes an earlier MOU and positions AMD as a core silicon partner in Rackspace's governed AI infrastructure stack, targeting regulated industries like healthcare. However, technical analysis shows fading momentum with MACD below its signal line, suggesting caution despite the stock's 334.93% gain over 12

WDC.headline: This Asian Country Is 1% The Size Of The US — And It Just Delivered 11x The S&P 500's Returns
WDC.description: South Korea's iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) has surged 112% year-to-date, delivering 11 times the return of the S&P 500. The rally is driven by SK Hynix and Samsung's dominance in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) manufacturing for AI accelerators, with both companies commanding nearly 46% of the ETF. Despite doubling in value, both stocks trade at low valuations with massive growth pipelines ahead.

WDC.headline: Dow Jones Hit Record Highs As Oil Drops To $77: Stock Market Today
WDC.description: U.S. stocks showed mixed performance on Tuesday as investors rotated out of technology into financials, utilities, and industrials. The Dow Jones reached record highs at 52,185.49, while the Nasdaq 100 declined 1.4%. Oil prices fell to $77/barrel on expectations of a Strait of Hormuz reopening deal. Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh prepares for his first policy meeting with markets pricing in a 97% probability of unchanged rates.

WDC.headline: S&P 500 Rally Faces Its First Real Test From the Fed
WDC.description: U.S. markets surged to record highs on Monday following President Trump's announcement of a completed Iran peace deal, with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all posting strong gains. Oil prices plummeted below $81 as the Strait of Hormuz reopened, benefiting airlines and transport while pressuring energy stocks. However, the rally is concentrated in semiconductor and tech names, with narrow breadth raising concerns about durability ahead of the Federal Reserve's Wednesday decision. SpaceX's IPO has

WDC.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Nasdaq Futures Fall, Dow Gains Amid US-Iran Deal— SpaceX, Dave And Buster's, Western Digital In Focus (UPDATED)
WDC.description: U.S. stock futures showed mixed performance on Tuesday following a preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement. The Dow Jones and Nasdaq 100 advanced while the S&P 500 fell slightly. Key movers included SpaceX surging with a market cap nearing $3 trillion, Dave and Buster's dropping 11.44% on weak earnings, Western Digital gaining 4.97% on analyst upgrade, and Paranovus Entertainment surging 30.07% on a direct offering announcement.

WDC.headline: Stock Market Today, June 15: Nvidia Jumps as Markets Soar on Peace Hopes
WDC.description: U.S. stock markets surged on June 15, 2026, driven by peace hopes between the U.S. and Iran, which caused oil prices to fall and boosted risk sentiment. The S&P 500 rose 1.65%, the Nasdaq jumped 3.07%, and the Dow gained 0.92% to a record close. Tech and semiconductor stocks led the rally, with Nvidia, Meta, Micron, and Western Digital posting strong gains. SpaceX surged nearly 20% following its IPO.

SPCX.headline: Forget SpaceX at $200. Buy This Space ETF Instead for Just $34.
SPCX.description: Following SpaceX's IPO at $135, the stock has surged to around $191, but its steep valuation and IPO volatility make it risky for conservative investors. The Ark Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) offers a diversified alternative at $34, providing exposure to SpaceX and other space industry leaders like Rocket Lab, L3Harris, and Advanced Micro Devices with lower concentration risk.

SPCX.headline: Maybe It's Time To Rethink How The S&P 500 Is constructed
SPCX.description: The article discusses potential reforms to S&P 500 inclusion methodology, noting that the index doesn't always contain the 500 largest publicly traded U.S. companies. Academic research by Arnott and Wu suggests alternative methodologies based on market cap could deliver similar performance while addressing concerns about subjective profitability requirements that exclude large unprofitable companies like SpaceX.

SPCX.headline: Want to Invest in SpaceX? These 2 Unstoppable ETFs Could Be Your Ticket.
SPCX.description: SpaceX has begun public trading following a historic IPO. While the S&P 500 has a 12-month waiting period before adding SpaceX, other indexes will include it sooner. The Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) and Invesco QQQ ETF (QQQ) are recommended as two effective ways to gain indirect exposure to SpaceX, with expected weightings around 3% in each fund.

SPCX.headline: SpaceX ETF Frenzy Reaches 'Bonkers' Levels As Volume Surges Past $1 Billion
SPCX.description: Following SpaceX's IPO, ten leveraged ETFs launched on Monday, generating over $1 billion in trading volume. Multiple issuers including ProShares, Tradr, Leverage Shares, Defiance, GraniteShares, REX Shares, and Direxion rushed to capitalize on investor demand for amplified exposure to SPCX. A fee war emerged among competitors, with expense ratios ranging from 0.75% to 2.20%. The existing Tema Space Innovators ETF also saw significant inflows of $146 million on Monday.

SPCX.headline: The SpaceX IPO Could Trigger a Massive Rotation Across AI Stocks. Here's Why.
SPCX.description: SpaceX's record $1.77 trillion IPO on June 12 is drawing investor capital away from AI stocks. The article argues SpaceX's 139x revenue valuation is unsustainably high for an unprofitable company, and predicts its eventual decline could trigger a rotation back to AI leaders like Nvidia and Broadcom. Recent declines in major AI stocks suggest investors are taking profits to fund SpaceX purchases. The outcome will also influence upcoming IPOs from OpenAI and Anthropic.

SPCX.headline: Move Over, Bitcoin: New SpaceX ETF Just Doubled IBIT's Historic Debut
SPCX.description: Following SpaceX's IPO, the Leverage Shares 2X Long SPCX Daily ETF (SPCH) generated $1.3 billion in day-two trading volume, surpassing Bitcoin Trust's previous record of $500 million. However, the article warns that leveraged ETFs like SPCH are designed for short-term trading only, as daily rebalancing creates volatility decay that can result in losses even when the underlying stock breaks even over time.

SPCX.headline: SpaceX Had the Biggest IPO in History. That Doesn't Mean You Should Buy the Stock.
SPCX.description: SpaceX's record-breaking $75 billion IPO and 20% first-day surge have generated significant hype, but the article cautions that size alone isn't a reason to invest. While the company offers ambitious growth potential in space exploration, AI, and satellite communications, it faces substantial risks including lack of profitability, extreme valuation metrics (P/S ratio of 117), technological uncertainty, governance concerns with Elon Musk's control, and dependence on government contracts. The stoc

SPCX.headline: JP Morgan's David Kelly Says It's 'A Great Economy For Stocks' Amid AI-Led Bull Market Despite Inflation Risks
SPCX.description: JPMorgan's David Kelly states the current environment is excellent for stocks despite inflation concerns, citing AI spending and resilient consumption as key drivers. He warns of concentration risk in the market, particularly in AI-related sectors, and suggests the next bear market could center on the most hyped sector. JPMorgan Asset Management recommends diversifying into defensive alternatives like real estate, transportation, and infrastructure.

SPCX.headline: OpenAI's Financials Were Just Leaked -- You Won't Believe How Much the Company Is Losing
SPCX.description: Leaked audited financials reveal OpenAI's operating losses surged 138% to nearly $21 billion in 2025, despite revenue growing 253% to $13.07 billion. R&D spending more than doubled to $19.18 billion and sales/marketing grew fivefold to $5.73 billion. The company is not expected to turn a profit until at least 2030 and faces challenges ahead of its planned IPO as competitors like Anthropic approach profitability.

SPCX.headline: Nasdaq Just Landed the Largest IPO in History. Here's Why the Exchange Stock Could Keep Winning.
SPCX.description: SpaceX's $75 billion IPO on Nasdaq positions the exchange to benefit from increased listing fees, data sales, and licensing revenues. The exchange's technology focus attracted SpaceX and could draw other major tech IPOs like OpenAI and Anthropic. Nasdaq offers conservative investors a profitable alternative to buying volatile IPOs directly.
